primary_accounting_issue: Appréciation du critère SPPI d’un actif financier lorsqu’une clause contractuelle peut modifier le calendrier ou le montant des flux de trésorerie en cas d’événement déclencheur.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule by selecting IFRS 9 as the single governing lineage because it directly governs classification and SPPI assessment for financial assets, and no higher-precedence overlapping document in the provided context addresses this same eligibility question.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low — IFRS 9 explicitly addresses contractual cash flow characteristics, contingent features and the resulting classification outcomes.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether contractual cash flows with contingent modifications can still be SPPI and how that affects classification.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify when interest still reflects a basic lending arrangement, including modified time value of money features.
These examples illustrate contingent and indexed features that do or do not remain consistent with SPPI.
These paragraphs complete the classification model by identifying the residual FVTPL outcome and the fair value option.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address insurance contract cash flows and discounting, not SPPI classification of financial assets.
These paragraphs concern hedge accounting rather than the SPPI test for classifying financial assets under IFRS 9.
These paragraphs concern earnings per share and potential ordinary shares, not financial asset classification.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets that satisfy SPPI and are held to collect contractual cash flows
Financial assets that satisfy SPPI and are held both to collect contractual cash flows and to sell
Financial assets that do not fall into the SPPI-based categories or are designated at fair value

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies amortised cost as a top-level measurement category when the asset meets SPPI and is held in a hold-to-collect business model.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies FVOCI as a top-level measurement category when the asset meets SPPI and is held in a collect-and-sell business model.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies FVTPL as the residual top-level measurement category when the SPPI-based categories are not met or when the fair value option is used.
